questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Number of reducting sugar:
(1) Glucose
(2) Galactose
(3) Maltose
(4) Lactose
(5) Sucrose
(6) Cellulose
(7) Starch
(8) Ribose
questions.solution: All the mono saccharides are reducing in nature
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The sum of total number of moles of $\mathrm{PhNHNH}_2$ used with $\mathrm{A} \& \mathrm{~B}$..........
questions.solution:
(A) has no $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{OH}$ group, $\alpha$ to the $\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{O}$ group. So it forms only phenylhydrazone with 1 mole of $\mathrm{PhNHNH}_2$.
(B) has ( $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{OH}$ ) group $\alpha$ to the $\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{O}$ group, so it forms only ozazone by reaction with 3 mole of $\mathrm{PhNHNH}_2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
